item.title: Our Students Won First Prize in the 7th Structural Design Competition
item.description: The results of the 7th Structural Design Competition of the School of Civil Engineering have been announced. The student team "凡事不要太绝队" guided by our group won the First Prize.
item.content:encoded:
The results of the 7th Structural Design Competition of the School of Civil Engineering have been announced. The student team "凡事不要太绝队" guided by our group won the First Prize.

Team Information:

  • Team Name: 凡事不要太绝队
  • Project: 就是209号
  • Captain: He Junyan
  • Members: He Junyan, Jiang Yihan, Gong Daxuan
  • Supervisor: Zhengshou Lai
  • Affiliation: School of Civil Engineering, Sun Yat-sen University
Award Certificate

Competition Information:

  • Competition: 7th Structural Design Competition, School of Civil Engineering, Sun Yat-sen University
  • Award: First Prize
  • Date: April 2026

Competition Theme​

The theme of this year's competition was "Structural Design and Fabrication of a Lele Cart Model". Inspired by the traditional Lele cart from the northern grasslands of China, participating teams were required to design and fabricate a model cart that could complete a cargo transportation task on a complex track — including slopes, curves, speed bumps, and single-span bridges — under the traction of a powered mini-car. The model material was limited to bamboo (bamboo strips and rods), and the cargo consisted of glass-bead-filled fabric bags (500g each). Teams had to independently choose the number of bags (20–40), balancing transportation time, task completion rate, and cost efficiency, presenting a comprehensive challenge to the model's dynamic load-bearing capacity, stability, and material economy.

Model Weighing — 121.4g
Model Load Testing

The project "就是209号" by "凡事不要太绝队" featured a truss-frame chassis weighing only 121.4g. Through lightweight design while maintaining load-bearing capacity, the model performed excellently in the loading test and ultimately won the First Prize.

item.title: Science Outreach and Recruitment at Jiaoling High School
item.description: The research group visited Jiaoling High School for their anniversary celebration, delivering science lectures and recruitment presentations.
item.content:encoded:
The research group visited Jiaoling High School for their anniversary celebration, delivering science lectures and recruitment presentations.

Background​

Jiaoling High School is one of the key feeder schools for Sun Yat-sen University. Invited by the school, our team conducted science lectures and recruitment promotions, introducing high school students to the frontier developments in civil engineering and the distinctive features of Sun Yat-sen University.

Activities​

  1. Science Outreach: Face-to-face communication with students, introducing the new directions of civil engineering, including smart construction, digital twins, AI for Science, and other cutting-edge fields.

  2. Recruitment Presentation: Introducing the academic directions, faculty, research platforms, and admission policies of the School of Civil Engineering at Sun Yat-sen University, answering students' questions about major selection and future development.

  3. Establishing Connections: Leaving various contact information and establishing communication channels for follow-up with prospective students.

  4. School Exchange: Meeting with the school principal to receive appreciation for our recruitment efforts and taking commemorative photos. During the event, we also met former SYSU Party Secretary Li Ping (an alumnus of Jiaoling), who encouraged and praised our school's outreach work.

Significance​

By going into high schools and reaching out to students, we help more outstanding students understand the charm and prospects of civil engineering, contributing to attracting quality students and expanding the university's influence.

item.title: Research Group Students Win Excellence Award in SAM Domestic CAE Software Competition
item.description: The student team "Jiu Tan" from our research group won the Excellence Award in the first SAM Domestic Structural Analysis Software Application Competition.
item.content:encoded:
The student team "Jiu Tan" from our research group won the Excellence Award in the first SAM Domestic Structural Analysis Software Application Competition.

Team Information:

  • Team Name: Jiu Tan (玖探队)
  • Members: Lu Yuzhou, Liu Mingjing, Chen Jianying
  • Advisor: Lai Zhengshou
  • Institution: School of Civil Engineering, Sun Yat-sen University

Research Content: Their work "Comparative Study of Static and Modal Analysis of Offshore Wind Turbine Structures Using SAM and Abaqus" established a finite element model of an offshore wind turbine including tower, nacelle, hub, and simplified blades. Static and modal analyses were conducted using both SAM and Abaqus for comparison.

Offshore wind turbine geometry model

Results showed high consistency between the two software across 9 typical working conditions. In static analysis, the relative error of tower top displacement ranged from 2.1% to 3.5%, and the relative error of maximum equivalent stress ranged from 1.8% to 3.2%. In modal analysis, the relative error of natural frequencies for all modes was less than 1.5%. These findings validate the engineering applicability of the domestic CAE software SAM for wind turbine structural analysis.

Stress and displacement contour comparison under typical working conditions

Competition Background:

  • Competition Name: First SAM Domestic Structural Analysis Software Application Competition (also known as "Aulento Cup" Domestic Industrial CAE Software Innovation Competition)
  • Organizer: China Shipbuilding Aulento (Beijing) Technology Co., Ltd.
  • Date: March 2026
  • Format: Online submission and review
  • Participants: Universities, research institutes, and enterprises nationwide
  • Scale: 103 teams registered
  • Awards: 1 First Prize, 2 Second Prizes, 3 Third Prizes, 14 Excellence Awards

Objectives: Promote independent development of domestic industrial software, validate SAM software's accuracy and applicability in complex engineering problems, cultivate domestic CAE software application talents, and facilitate wider adoption of domestic structural analysis software in engineering practice.

More info: Finalists Announced | First "Aulento Cup" Domestic Industrial CAE Software Innovation Competition

item.title: APAAM lab website is live
item.description: The APAAM lab website is now online.
item.content:encoded:
The APAAM lab website is now online.

This site showcases our research directions, publications, team members, and student recruitment information.

More content coming soon.

item.title: Our Students Won Second Prize in the 1st Digital Pool Competition
item.description: The student team guided by our group won the Second Prize (Numerical Group) in the 1st "Hailutong Cup" Digital Pool Competition and Pioneer Intelligent Computing Special Session, Sun Yat-sen University Internal Competition.
item.content:encoded:
The student team guided by our group won the Second Prize (Numerical Group) in the 1st "Hailutong Cup" Digital Pool Competition and Pioneer Intelligent Computing Special Session, Sun Yat-sen University Internal Competition.

Team Information:

  • Members: Liu Mingjing, Zhang Haoqin, Zhang Biao
  • Supervisor: Zhengshou Lai
  • Affiliation: School of Civil Engineering, Sun Yat-sen University
Award Certificate

Competition Information:

  • Competition: 1st "Hailutong Cup" Digital Pool Competition and Pioneer Intelligent Computing Special Session, Sun Yat-sen University Internal Competition
  • Award: Second Prize (Numerical Group)
  • Date: September 2025

Research Content​

The team conducted a numerical simulation study of ship-free-surface-particle multiphase flow using the KCS (KRISO Container Ship) standard hull model as the research object, based on the coupling of the Immersed Boundary Method (IBM) and the Discrete Element Method (DEM).

Using the Phynexis solver (interIBdem), the team simulated the motion response of the KCS hull model in a two-phase flow environment (water-air, surface tension coefficient 0.07 N/m), and introduced discrete particles through the DEM method to study the interaction between the ship and the particulate medium during navigation.

KCS Hull CFD-DEM Coupled Simulation Results (Free Surface and Pressure Field Distribution)

item.title: Our Students Won Second Prize in the 14th APMCM Mathematical Contest in Modeling
item.description: The student team guided by our group won the Second Prize (Undergraduate Group) in the 14th APMCM Asia and Pacific Mathematical Contest in Modeling.
item.content:encoded:
The student team guided by our group won the Second Prize (Undergraduate Group) in the 14th APMCM Asia and Pacific Mathematical Contest in Modeling.

Team Information:

  • Members: Zheng Zefeng, Mo Ziwei, Liang Weilin
  • Supervisor: Zhengshou Lai
  • Affiliation: School of Civil Engineering, Sun Yat-sen University
Award Certificate

Competition Information:

  • Competition: 14th APMCM Asia and Pacific Mathematical Contest in Modeling
  • Award: Second Prize (Undergraduate Group)
  • Date: October 2024

Competition Theme​

The team selected Problem D: Exploring Frontiers in Quantum-Accelerated AI. The problem focused on the intersection of quantum computing and artificial intelligence, requiring participants to transform classical machine learning problems into QUBO (Quadratic Unconstrained Binary Optimization) models and solve them using the simulated annealing algorithm provided by the SDK.

The team addressed three sub-problems:

  1. Cloud Computing Resource Demand Prediction — Based on 9 months of historical data, an AR (Autoregressive) time series model was built to predict October demand, which was then transformed into a QUBO model for solving;
  2. Iris Dataset Classification — The optimization problem of the SVM (Support Vector Machine) classifier was mapped into QUBO form to achieve quantum-accelerated classification;
  3. MNIST Handwritten Digit Recognition — A CNN (Convolutional Neural Network) model was constructed, with an attempt to transform its training process into a QUBO optimization problem.

The study demonstrated that the QUBO model achieved good solving performance on the first two tasks, validating the potential of quantum computing in AI optimization problems.

item.title: Joined School of Civil Engineering, Sun Yat-sen University
item.description: Dr. Zhengshou Lai joined the School of Civil Engineering, Sun Yat-sen University as an Associate Professor.
item.content:encoded:
Dr. Zhengshou Lai joined the School of Civil Engineering, Sun Yat-sen University as an Associate Professor.

Dr. Zhengshou Lai officially joined the School of Civil Engineering, Sun Yat-sen University as an Associate Professor in March 2023. Prior to this, he received his Ph.D. in Civil Engineering from Clemson University, USA in 2018, conducted postdoctoral research at the School of Intelligent Engineering, Sun Yat-sen University from 2019 to 2021, and pursued joint postdoctoral research at the Hong Kong University of Science and Technology and Sun Yat-sen University under the Hong Kong Scholars Program from 2021 to 2023.

At the School of Civil Engineering, Dr. Lai continues his research in computational mechanics and multiscale simulation of granular materials, with research interests including discrete element methods, fluid-solid coupling multiscale simulation, and machine learning and data science. He is also a dual-appointed faculty member at the Research Center for Applied Mathematics, Hong Kong Advanced Research Institute.

Students interested in computational mechanics are welcome to apply to join our group!
